title: Fantasy FIFA World Cup 2026 team previews: Japan
original_text:
World Cup Live. FPL Coming Soon. Lowest Price This Year

The FFScout Podcast is a daily FPL podcast providing you the tips you need to succeed. Featuring FPL Harry, Joe, Neale, Sam, FPL General and more!

Win prizes and try to beat the FFScout community!

Ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup Fantasy campaign, we’re taking a closer look at each nation competing at this summer’s tournament – highlighting their recent form, qualifying matches, key players and more.

Japan became the first nation outside of the host countries to officially qualify for the tournament and will now prepare for an eighth consecutive World Cup appearance.

Stability has also played a major role in their success. Hajime Moriyasu has spent eight years in charge of the national side, helping build a well-drilled and tactically flexible squad capable of competing with some of the world’s strongest nations.

Recent years have shown exactly how dangerous Japan can be on the biggest stage. They famously came from 2-0 down to beat Brazil for the first time in their history and also secured an impressive victory over England at Wembley. During recent World Cups, they have beaten both Spain and Germany, while also reaching the round of 16 in three of the last four tournaments.

Hajime Moriyasu announced his final squad for the upcoming World Cup on May 15.

Star player Kaoru Mitoma picked up an injury ahead of the tournament and ultimately missed out on Japan’s final squad. The Brighton & Hove Albion winger has played a huge role in the Samurai Blue’s recent success, so his absence is a major blow to the side.

As already highlighted, Japan became the first nation outside of the host countries to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup after producing an excellent qualifying campaign.

The Samurai Blue won an outstanding 12 of their 15 matches while scoring a remarkable 54 goals along the way. They also kept 12 clean sheets, underlining just how strong they looked at both ends of the pitch throughout the qualifiers.

Their only defeat came against Australia, although Japan heavily rotated for that match and fielded what was effectively a third-string side.

An average of 3.4 goals per 90 minutes comfortably exceeded every other nation in the qualification campaign. And 0.2 goals conceded per match was also a confederation-low.

The standout attacker when looking at Japan’s output during qualifying was forward Ayase Ueda ($7.0m). The Feyenoord striker scored eight goals during the campaign and converted an impressive third of his total attempts on target.

He led the way for xG, too, and scored a penalty.

Koki Ogawa ($4.9m) also produced excellent numbers despite far more limited minutes. He played less than half the gametime of Ueda but still managed to score six times. However, he may need to settle for a role off the bench during the tournament itself.

Meanwhile, Crystal Palace midfielder Daichi Kamada ($5.8m) found the net five times during qualifying, which proved enough to secure his place in the final squad. The same can’t be said for Takumi Minamino though. Despite scoring four goals during the campaign, he ultimately failed to make the cut.

Defender Ritsu Doan ‘s ($5.1m) figures also pop out: 22 shots, three goals and a squad-second-best 3.89 xG.

Takefusa Kubo ($7.0m) led the way creatively during qualifying. The Real Sociedad playmaker produced a huge 30 key passes, with set pieces and corners also contributing heavily to that total. From those chances created, he registered eight assists, although that surprisingly wasn’t the highest total in the squad.

That honour instead went to 33-year-old veteran Junya Ito ($5.0m), who produced an outstanding 10 assists in just 446 minutes of action. Despite such an impressive qualifying campaign, Ito could still find himself used mainly as an impact option from the bench during the tournament.

Doan is prominent in the above table, too, with 13 key passes.

Whilst their opening friendlies of 2025 produced mixed results, with Japan failing to beat Mexico, the United States, and Paraguay.

However, the Samurai Blue quickly rediscovered their rhythm. Victories over fellow World Cup nations Ghana and Scotland impressed, but it was the stunning 3-2 comeback win against Brazil and the 1-0 victory over England at Wembley Stadium that really grabbed attention.

Japan now head into the tournament full of confidence and carrying strong momentum. Unbeaten in eight, they’ve won their last five matches to nil.

It’s a tricky start for Moriyasu’s troops, who face the group favourites. Given Japan’s form and the Netherlands’ capacity to implode, however, you wouldn’t be too surprised if the bookies’ odds were upset.

Thereafter, Japan become seriously good Fantasy targets.

A beleaguered Tunisia side and a Sweden outfit that concedes for fun follow.

We saw just how strong Japan looked defensively during the qualification campaign, and in subsequent friendlies, so their backline could offer a good chance of clean sheets from Round 2 onwards – perhaps even before.

The Samurai Blue operate with a back-three system, which gives their wing-backs far more freedom to push forward and contribute in attack.

One of those is Ritsu Doan ($5.1m). More of a winger at club level, he registered 18 Bundesliga attacking returns for Freiburg in 2024/25, earning himself a move to Eintracht Frankfurt last summer.

No Japan player bettered his expected goal involvement (xGI) figure of 5.29 in qualification, while his shot map shows the types of dangerous areas he’s capable of occupying:

Fellow wing-back Keito Nakamura ($5.6m) has been listed as a midfielder, which dents his appeal. He did deliver four attacking returns in his last six friendly appearances, however, so is still a valid pick even without the clean sheets.

Among Japan’s other midfield options, Takefusa Kubo ($7.0m) stands out as arguably the most appealing pick, especially with Mitoma ruled out.

Set pieces played a major role in Kubo producing a huge 30 key passes during qualifying, comfortably more than any of his teammates. The Real Sociedad star also delivered strong end product, scoring four goals and registering eight assists during the AFC campaign.

Leading the line is striker Ayase Ueda ($7.0m), who scored eight goals during the qualification campaign and firmly established himself as Japan’s biggest attacking threat heading into the tournament.

Ueda also takes penalties for his country, which only increases his appeal even further from a World Cup Fantasy perspective.

He comes into the tournament off the back of a 25-goal season with Feyenoord, too.

Early Thoughts: Morocco will win group C - Sorry Scotland fans! Croatia will top Group L - Sorry England fans!

Agree with Morocco, think England will top their group though.

Kounde off injured, apparently. Probably just a precaution.

How does this look? Rochet Ordonez Ryerson Elvedi Olise Bruno Sabitzer Wirtz Mbappe Cristiano Oyarsabel Vargas Mendez Diaz Mojica Haaland 12th man

Really close to being done here… three spots up for grabs, both keepers and the Heggem CB spot, with 12.3m to spend on the 3 spots… (0.6itb below) RANGEL, VARGAS Kimmich, Cucu, HEGGEM, Mojica, Ordonez Olise, Bruno, Wirtz, Musiala, James Mbappe, Haaland, Oyerzabal Plan to do 12th man Vini Jr in MW2 and do Musiala, Haaland > Raphinha, Kane. WC in MW3.

Olise was 22.9% owned at the start of this match Needed a Yamal replacement anyway

The boy is just too good! Lucky to have him at Bayern.

Olise is just on another level, saw it when watching him play for us, and now for Bayern and France national. We supposed to get a massive sell on fee if Bayern sell him, question is if teams like Real Madrid can stump up that kind of offer (and Bayern accept) Either way has to be in the World Cup Fantasy team for sure

Repost Maybe a completely random nation will lift the world cup trophy next month. I really think it will be a chaotic but fun filled world cup as usual. If FIFA won't be corrupt in matches like they are with tickets to games. Just as flawed as UEFA. WINNERS Brain says Brazil or France Heart says Germany Dark horses Austria, Colombia, South Korea, Algeria No Spain, Argentina, Portugal, England. What do I know?!

Alright, lads, how would you improve this team with 0.7 itb? Crepeau Doue | Gabriel | Kimmich | Cucurella McGinn (vc) | Olise | Doku | Musiala Cunha (c) | Oyarzabal Rochet, Mbappe, Nuno Mendes, Bruno 12th man Haaland

McGinn + Doku are nice sleeper picks. Cunha, however, is a tad too much.

Who’s the most assure starter in Brazil’s defense; Danilo or Douglas Santos?

I saw a Cassowary in the wild the other day!

No they don’t. I also saw six salties on the Daintree river, if that helps?

The Daintree rainforest is the last living remnant of Gondwana, the oldest surviving rainforest on Earth. Amazon shmamazon!

Japan have a quality starting XI. They could win group F and have a great chance of making the quarter finals if so.

Anyone else struggling to spend all their funds in GW 1?

I'm targeting Spain, Portugal and Jerries wk 1. A couple of

Probably 3? Im happy with - 3 Spain vs Saudi -2 Porras vs Uzb -3 Jerries vs Ivory coast -2/3 Norway vs Senegal -1/2 Colombia vs Congo France vs Iraq and Brazil vs Haiti is the problem

I'm unsure on Germany. Have pretty much same setup, possibly different players. Happy with GK-DEF-FWD and MD2 plans. Just need to try different combos with midfielders.

Germany has one of the easiest fixtures in MD1.

Haaland and wirtz > mbappe and raphina is the plan. Vini 12th man. Would mean keeping kimmich and havertz which isnt that bad i guess.

No need to. Some decent cheap enablers. Have Rangel, Cakir, Mwene, Ryerson, Widmer for now. Hopefully predicted lineups are fairly accurate. Not like PL or UCL picking players that won't play for a week.

Germany vs Ivory coast is not tricky. These African sides don't do CS.

Trickier than Curacao. Ivory Coast aren't that bad as some reports and Germany aren't as good as some reports.

Ivory Coast conceded... 0 goals in qualifying.

They played an indian ocean island plus Gabon, Gambia, Kenya and Burunidi... Big difference

Ivory Coast starting XI is really physical and athletic, Germany will struggle against them. Both CBs are massive beasts and Ndicka scored 5 goals this season. Yan Diomandé averaged an attack return every 104 minutes in the Germany league + cup.

Is the 5% limit for scouting bonus based on the Matchday deadline % ownership or the % ownership when you buy the player?

will be deadline ownership or worst case scenario, on the day they play ownership. Its FIFA afterall!

If it's the same as the Club World Cup, it'll be the day they play!

Remember to have your team ready 1-2 hours before the actual deadline. Re: Club World Cup Fantasy

Me. I reckon I'm going with 2 from Gunn/Rochet/Vargas

Would be typical if one of these African sides come to the party. Mane will prob be highest scorer wk 1.

Apparently I have no idea what I'm doing because my team looks absolutely nothing like any draft I've seen here. Galindez Pickford Sanchez Rodriguez Doan Cucurella Mendes Raphinha Olmo Olise Fernandes Arias Alvarez Ueda Kane Basically, Japan are a cheat code. They are a really good team. They beat Brazil and England in recent friendlies. Good in defense, good in attack. I think they win their group comfortably even over Netherlands. Doan is the best player in the game as far as I'm concerned. He plays RW for Japan and has the 2nd highest xG on the team and yet he's priced as a 5.1m defender?! Obviously less than 5% owned as well. I have captaincy rotation among my frontline. Pick good players, worry about rotation later and/or WC3.

Based on formation in recent friendlies, Doan is playing as RWB. Its a different team. England are not as popular and Rodriguez is preferred over Arias. Thats my two cents.

Arias and Rodriguez both started the last friendly and Arias scored 2.

Japan are a wait and see for knockouts for me. Good results qualifying from Asia and in friendlies but the fun stops here 😉 Netherlands, Sweden, Tunisia not in great form but it's not as cleansheet friendly for Japan as other groups with lesser teams. Without hindsight Doan looks overpriced in that group despite attacking threat.

Plus Japan heavily rotates it's roster during international tournaments. Re: last Asia Cup of Nations.

Japan's group is not being the easiest is probably the reason people are ignoring it.

We'll get snared by Curacao, Cape Verde, Jordan etc getting a goal in a defeat that wrecks a clean sheet certainty.

That's why you go triple attack vs one of those.

Yeah I suppose that's the main annoying thing with Japan. But I think they win the group. I'm going to keep Doan but I think it's harder to justify Ueda.

How does the wildcard work in this fantasy world cup? "Will allow you to make unlimited transfers within a specific round (cannot be used for the first match of the group stage or for the round of 32)." So this means I can make as many transfers as I want on matchday 2, if I use it. Is this temporary, or will the team swapped this way stay for matchday 3 like in fantasy PL?

Same as the regular wildcard. You make a new team in round 2 and that will be your team in round 3 as well (before making transfers). Remember the wildcard window is quite short (only 14 hours between round 1 ending and round 2 starting).

Musiala. Havertz takes up a forward slot and I think there are 3 better ones to choose from.

Hence the dilemma. Could even ignore musiala and get sane instead and save some bucks. But haverts has the form. Great cap option.

Didn't know.Morocco was declared the winner of AFCON 2026. I remember all the controversy with the Senegal team walking out and then winning it on extra time, but not this fact.

Yeah, sadly seems to be a lot of corruption around the african federation. Can't say FIFA is barely better to be fair

Tweaked draft. Anything worth changing? Rangel Kimmich cucu ryerson Olise Bruno Wirtz Doku Haaland oyarzabal havertz Rochet jrod muni mojica

Looks good. I have a very similar draft, but with Mbap and Haaland(12th man).

Hard to make a bad draft with the given player pool. All comes down to chip strategy and luck.

It will be the same as every WC game. You need to be ahead and get lucky by the time you get to the knockout round as the player pool gets smaller and smaller. Teams will be identical and at a certain stage it will be inpossible to better your rank.

maybe we should plan to use 3pts hits to attack the fixtures harder? Might be worth it with additional bonus flying around.

Taking -3/-6 in a short form.tournament like this is very likely a huge rank killer. I'd rather set a round 1 team and then WC to a round 2 & 3 hydrid.

Difference could come down to when we decide to stick or twist the captaincy.

That is why maybe having a lot of captains is not such a bad idea. Obviously not 15, but a good middle ground like 8-10 meaning only max 2 players of one team vs 3.
